column8: Major dam on the Bouregreg river; potential water resource of 687 Mm³/year; elevation works aim to increase potential to 1200 Mm³.
Located about 20 km south of Sidi Slimane; irrigates 28,700 ha; provides flood protection and generates 33 million kWh annually.
One of three major dams in the region, used for electricity generation and irrigation of the Gharb plains.
Renewable groundwater resource covering 390 km² with 126 Mm³/year; important for irrigation and has a balanced hydrological budget.
Large free aquifer covering ~4000 km²; replenished by precipitation; 134 Mm³/year of renewable water.
Groundwater resource covering 350 km² with 17 Mm³/year of potential; water table depth varies between 10 m to 30 m.
Extension of the Maâmora aquifer covering 200 km²; potential recharge of 7.5 Mm³/year; depth varies between 20 and 60 m.
Small aquifer (~15 km²) in Mâaziz, fed by the Tanoubert river; important local source of groundwater.
